phase1.explanation:
Packages organize classes into namespaces:

Package declaration:
• package com.mycompany.util;
• Must be FIRST line in file
• Follows reverse domain name convention

Import statements:
• import com.mycompany.util.MathUtils; — imports specific class
• import com.mycompany.util.*; — imports all classes in package
• java.lang is automatically imported

Why use packages:
• Avoid naming conflicts
• Organize code logically
• Control access (protected/package-private)

Common built-in packages:
• java.lang — String, Math, System (auto-imported)
• java.util — Scanner, ArrayList, HashMap
• java.io — File, BufferedReader
• java.time — LocalDate, LocalDateTime

Fully Qualified Name: package.Class (without import)
• Example: java.util.ArrayList<String>()

phase3.prompt:
Create a package structure:
• com.example.shapes
• In it, create Rectangle and Circle classes with area() methods
• In a Main class (can be in same file), import and use both shapes

Note: For simplicity, we'll put everything in one file with package declarations.
dialogueHints: Use package com.example.shapes; in both shape classes
Main can be in default package or different package
Import both classes and use them
